title: add helper for discovering and aliasing system architecture
summary: Multiple Canonical charms duplicate the same platform.processor()/platform.machine() architecture-detection-and-aliasing snippet (x86_64->amd64, aarch64->arm64). cos-lib itself already contains a partial copy of this logic inside CosTool._get_tool_path (src/cosl/cos_tool.py:184-186). The ask is to add a single shared helper (e.g. cosl.utils.get_host_architecture()) that returns a canonical alias, and have cos_tool.py use it, so charms can import one common implementation.
why_good_first_issue: Self-contained, no architecture knowledge needed: write one small pure function plus a couple of mock-based unit tests. Labelled 'good first issue' by maintainers, unclaimed, no comments, no competing PR (the related PR #139 only patches cos_tool.py, not a helper). Clear 'done': helper exists, is unit-tested, and cos_tool.py calls it.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'cd ~/work/cos-lib && grep -n "platform.machine" src/cosl/cos_tool.py && sed -n "183,187p" src/cosl/cos_tool.py' ; also grep -rn "def.*arch|architecture" src  (returns nothing -> no helper exists).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: src/cosl/cos_tool.py:184-186 inlines arch detection (arch = platform.machine(); arch = 'amd64' if arch == 'x86_64' else arch) and 'grep -rn def.*arch|architecture src' finds NO shared helper, so the duplicated logic the issue describes is still present and unextracted.
fix_hint: Add get_host_architecture() to src/cosl/utils.py mapping x86_64->amd64 and aarch64/arm64/armv8b/armv8l->arm64 (mirror craft-parts get_host_architecture); replace the inline lines 184-185 in cos_tool.py with a call to it; add mock-based tests in tests/test_cos_tool.py (or a new tests/test_utils.py).
notes: Closely related to the self-identified aarch64 bug below and to open PR #139 ('Fix tool path for arm64'), which is only a partial fix and explicitly references this issue. Implementing the helper would also fix that bug. NOTE: other 'good first issue' labelled issues (#145 Nginx resolver, #100 interface.py tests, #119 S3ConnectionInfo) are now OBSOLETE in cos-lib because the whole coordinated_workers module (nginx.py, interface.py, S3ConnectionInfo) was moved out to canonical/cos-coordinated-workers in commit #146; coordinated_workers/__init__.py now just raises ImportError.

title: CosTool fails to locate cos-tool binary on arm64 (aarch64 not aliased to arm64)
summary: CosTool._get_tool_path (src/cosl/cos_tool.py:184-186) aliases x86_64->amd64 but does NOT alias aarch64->arm64. On arm64 hosts platform.machine() returns 'aarch64', so the code looks for a file named 'cos-tool-aarch64'. The binary released/shipped is 'cos-tool-arm64', so the lookup fails, CosTool silently disables itself (path=None, _disabled=True), and juju topology label injection is skipped on arm64.
why_good_first_issue: One-line fix (add aarch64->arm64 to the alias map) plus one mock-based unit test mirroring the existing tests. Fully reproducible without a deployment. Clear, narrow, low-risk. Good for a newcomer to land a real bug fix with a test.
repro_steps: On VM cos: multipass exec cos -- git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/cos-lib ~/work/cos-lib; cd ~/work/cos-lib; with platform.machine mocked to 'aarch64', construct CosTool(default_query_type='promql') and inspect .path / ._disabled. Script: with unittest.mock.patch('platform.machine', lambda: 'aarch64'): tool = CosTool(...); assert tool.path is None. Run via: PYTHONPATH=src uv run --frozen --isolated --extra=dev python repro_arch.py. (Existing test tests/test_cos_tool.py only ever mocks x86_64 / teakettle, never aarch64.)
repro_result: confirmed: with platform.machine mocked to 'aarch64', CosTool searches for 'cos-tool-aarch64', tool.path is None and tool._disabled is True (binary on disk is cos-tool-amd64; released arm64 binary is cos-tool-arm64). Matches the real-world log in open PR #139: 'Could not locate cos-tool at: "cos-tool-aarch64"'. Baseline tox -e unit = 132 passed, 89% cov, env builds in ~18s.
fix_hint: In src/cosl/cos_tool.py change the alias logic to also map arm: e.g. arch = {'x86_64':'amd64','aarch64':'arm64'}.get(arch, arch) (or reuse the new get_host_architecture helper from issue #29). Add a test @unittest.mock.patch('platform.machine', lambda: 'aarch64') asserting the path ends with 'cos-tool-arm64'. Open PR #139 already implements this map for cos_tool.py and loki_logger.py but is stalled/unmerged.
notes: Distinct from issue #29 (which is the broader 'add a shared helper' cleanup); this row is the concrete, reproducible bug. PR #139 is the existing (unmerged) attempt and notes it is only a partial fix because CosTool is still copy-pasted across several charm libs.
